summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting on January 26, 2021. Key business included approving road mile certifications, personnel actions including a resignation and a separation of a probationary firefighter, amending an IT contract, and authorizing pre-disciplinary proceedings against a firefighter paramedic following an executive session. The newly elected Clermont County Prosecutor Mark Dekovey introduced himself to the board.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda - passed
Motion to approve payment of bills for January 13–26, 2021 - passed (Trustee Hodges abstained due to conflict of interest)
Motion to approve certificate of road miles (46.626 miles) for Clermont County Engineer for 2021 - passed
Motion to accept resignation of part-time firefighter paramedic Mark Peters retro to December 17, 2020 - passed
Motion to release probationary part-time firefighter EMT Michael Krohn from employment effective 1-22-21 - passed
Motion to hire probationary part-time fitness instructor Paul Hilliard - passed
Motion to amend IT contract from $2,350/month to $2,560/month (originally approved January 12, 2021) - passed
Motion to enter executive session at 7:31 PM per ORC 121.22 regarding employee appointment/employment/compensation and bargaining sessions - passed
Motion to authorize Township Administrator/Fire Chief to initiate pre-disciplinary process for alleged misconduct of a Township Firefighter Paramedic which may result in discharge - passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:56 PM - passed
action_items: Steve Watkins to provide pricing for lighting/motion light and fan for columbarium shelter
Director Meadows to get road mile certificate signed and returned to Clermont County Engineer
Township Administrator to present redesigned website at the next trustees meeting
Township Administrator/Fire Chief to initiate pre-disciplinary process for a firefighter paramedic
Continue search for part-time zoning inspector
Follow up on Gaynor Road issue
Chief Pegram/Harry Holberg to reach out to Attorney General and EPA regarding Combs property
key_amounts: 46.626 road miles certified for 2021 (approximately 93 lane miles)
notable_topics: Introduction of newly elected Clermont County Prosecutor Mark Dekovey
Certification of 46.626 road miles for 2021 with three new road additions
Personnel actions: resignation of Mark Peters, separation of Michael Krohn, hiring of Paul Hilliard
Columbarium shelter improvements - lighting and fan installation
Combs property pursuit requiring coordination with AG and EPA
Seven new fire department recruits onboarded
Zoning department 2020 permit recap - exceeded 2019 numbers
Authorization of pre-disciplinary process for a firefighter paramedic for alleged misconduct
First video-recorded meeting for the township
notes: This was noted as the first video-recorded Board of Trustees meeting for Goshen Township. Trustee Hodges abstained from the bill payment vote due to a conflict of interest involving a payment to his employer. The transcript contained significant portions of corrupted/garbled text and repeated boilerplate headers, limiting full extraction of all discussion details. The executive session lasted approximately 83 minutes (7:31 PM to 8:54 PM), which is notably long. A prayer was offered for a family member (Jack) connected to Goshen firefighters who appeared to be dealing with health or personal challenges.
title: 1.26.2021 BOT MTG
